title: [VPlan] Introduce m_c_Logical(And|Or)
author: artagnon

tests.test_body:
define void @constant_fold_commutative_and(ptr %ptr.n, ptr noalias %p, i1 %cond) {
entry:
  br label %ph

ph:                                               ; preds = %latch, %entry
  %iv = phi i64 [ 0, %entry ], [ %iv.next, %latch ]
  %n = load i64, ptr %ptr.n, align 4
  br i1 %cond, label %latch, label %pred.1

pred.1:                                           ; preds = %ph
  %cmp = icmp ult i64 %n, 4
  br i1 %cmp, label %latch, label %pred.2

pred.2:                                           ; preds = %pred.1
  %cmp.2 = icmp ult i64 %n, 7
  br label %latch

latch:                                            ; preds = %pred.2, %pred.1, %ph
  %merge.val = phi i1 [ false, %ph ], [ true, %pred.1 ], [ %cmp.2, %pred.2 ]
  %gep = getelementptr i8, ptr %p, i64 %iv
  store i1 %merge.val, ptr %gep, align 1
  %iv.next = add i64 %iv, 1
  %ec = icmp eq i64 %iv, 16
  br i1 %ec, label %exit, label %ph

exit:                                             ; preds = %latch
  ret void
}

comments.author: fhahn
comments.body: This now needs an update to main I think after https://github.com/llvm/llvm-project/pull/179426 landed

comments.author: artagnon
comments.body:
> This now needs an update to main I think after #179426 landed

I'm actually thinking of closing this one; it's impossible to cover the patch with any additional tests, as:
1. VPInstruction::LogicalAnd is created by VPlan with a definite operand order. I tried to test with swapped operands, but failed.
2. simplifyRecipe already simplifies select !c, x, y -> select c, y, x.

Why should we add extra code that cannot be covered? The benefit would be purely NFC, and would allow us to write m_c_Logical(And|Or) with swapped operand orders: what do you think?

comments.author: artagnon
comments.body:
> it should perform an additional fold for the test case below I think

Thanks for the test case!
